claimText: Regional Rural Banks (RRBs) were established in 1975 based on the recommendations of the Narasimham Working Group.
evidenceExcerpt: Established in 1975 based on Narasimham Working Group recommendations.
examUse: Statement-based question on the historical origin and committee recommendations for RRBs.

claimText: The ownership of Regional Rural Banks is jointly held by the Government of India (50%), the sponsoring commercial bank (35%), and the respective State Government (15%).
evidenceExcerpt: Ownership: GoI (50%), State Government (15%), Sponsoring Bank (35%).
examUse: Statement-based question on the shareholding pattern of RRBs.

claimText: Regional Rural Banks are regulated by the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) but supervised by the National Bank for Agriculture and Rural Development (NABARD).
evidenceExcerpt: Regulated by RBI and supervised by NABARD.
examUse: Statement-based question distinguishing the regulatory and supervisory authorities for RRBs.

claimText: For tax purposes, Regional Rural Banks are treated as cooperative societies under the Income Tax Act, 1961.
evidenceExcerpt: Treated as cooperative societies under the Income Tax Act, 1961.
examUse: Statement-based question on the legal and tax classification of RRBs.
distractorHooks: Treated as commercial companies for tax purposes

claimText: Regional Rural Banks are mandated to allocate 75% of their Adjusted Net Bank Credit (ANBC) to Priority Sector Lending (PSL).
evidenceExcerpt: Must allocate 75% of ANBC to Priority Sector Lending (PSL).
examUse: Comparison question on Priority Sector Lending targets between commercial banks and RRBs.

claimText: Regional Rural Banks are required to maintain a Capital to Risk-Weighted Assets Ratio (CRAR) of 9%.
evidenceExcerpt: Must maintain a Capital to Risk-Weighted Assets Ratio (CRAR) of 9%.
examUse: Statement-based question on capital adequacy requirements for RRBs.
